llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm (GitHub Copilot) is present in the study; control group had no AI assistance.
llm_extracted.notes: The paper reports task completion time in minutes (smaller is better). The treatment group completed the task 55.8% faster, which represents a reduction in time. The 95% CI [21%, 89%] refers to the percent reduction/improvement. Means are clearly reported: control = 160.89 min, copilot = 71.17 min. Multiple heterogeneous effect regression coefficients are reported but these are interaction terms, not the main effect, so they are not used for the primary log ratio calculation.
source_quotes: The treatment group, with access to the AI pair programmer, completed the task 55.8% faster than the control group.
the treated group completed the task 55.8% faster (95% confidence interval: 21-89%)
Conditioning on completing the task, the average completion time from the treated group is 71.17 minutes and 160.89 minutes for the control group.
This represents a 55.8% reduction in completion time.
The p-value for the t-test is 0.0017, and a 95% confidence interval for the improvement is between [21%, 89%].

computed.notes: The paper reports task completion time in minutes (smaller is better). The treatment group completed the task 55.8% faster, which represents a reduction in time. The 95% CI [21%, 89%] refers to the percent reduction/improvement. Means are clearly reported: control = 160.89 min, copilot = 71.17 min. Multiple heterogeneous effect regression coefficients are reported but these are interaction terms, not the main effect, so they are not used for the primary log ratio calculation. | SDs/SEs missing; variance not computable
